narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a white male applicant with an upper-middle-class family background, your law school application was carefully evaluated using a predictive model trained on data from thousands of students who took the bar exam. This model assesses the likelihood of passing the bar on the first try based on academic records, test scores, and other background factors, and unfortunately, it estimated a 73% probability that you would fail—high enough to lead to the rejection of your application. The decision hinged primarily on your Year 3 school decile ranking, which placed you in the 3rd decile (below the typical 5.53 average), strongly signaling to the model a heightened risk of bar exam struggles compared to most students. Your LSAT score of 31 also played a major role in pushing the prediction toward failure, as it falls well short of the dataset average of 36.7 seen among successful bar takers. Reinforcing this were your undergraduate GPA of 2.70, lower than the typical 3.21, and your family income bracket, which didn't provide enough of a counterbalance despite being upper-middle. On a more positive note, your full-time student status nudged things slightly toward success, aligning with the vast majority (over 92%) of students who pursue law this way, and your Year 1 decile of 5 (close to the average of 5.71) offered a minor boost. Overall, your profile stood out as riskier than typical applicants because your later academic decile and standardized test scores deviated more sharply downward from the norms that correlate with bar passage. While this is disappointing, especially given your dedication to full-time study, it doesn't define your potential—many students improve dramatically with targeted effort. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to 37 or higher, strengthening your undergraduate record through additional coursework if possible, and seeking mentorship to elevate your school performance into higher deciles. Law schools value persistence, so revising your application with these enhancements could open new doors. You're capable of turning this around—keep pushing.
